issue_title: [bug] tools/weather: unbounded HTTP read + silent chunked corruption + bare-spaces URL encoding lose chat or return wrong location
issue_body:
### Summary

`tools::weather::http_get` in `crates/genie-core/src/tools/weather.rs:182-228` has the **same unbounded-read pattern** that PR #174 (closes #173) just fixed in `ha::client::http_request`: a 10-second `TcpStream::connect` timeout, then **no timeout** on `writer.write_all`, the status-line / header `read_line`, or the body-accumulation loop. The body loop also has **no size cap**, so a slow or misbehaving Open-Meteo (or a man-in-the-middle on the plain-HTTP connection, since the comment at line 181 explicitly disables TLS) can hang the chat task forever or grow RSS without bound. Two adjacent bugs live in the same function family:

- The "chunked transfer encoding" handler at [weather.rs:216-225](crates/genie-core/src/tools/weather.rs#L216-L225) doesn't actually decode chunked encoding; it splits on `\r\n` and concatenates the non-hex-only lines **without a separator**, so chunked JSON spanning multiple chunks is silently corrupted. The detection heuristic also misfires when the response body legitimately begins with a hex-looking value.
- URL encoding at [weather.rs:71](crates/genie-core/src/tools/weather.rs#L71) only handles spaces (`location.replace(' ', "+")`). Reserved characters `&`, `?`, `#`, `=`, `%`, `+` pass through unescaped into the query string. A user asking the weather in *"Cafe & Bar Tokyo"* generates `name=Cafe+&+Bar+Tokyo&count=1…` — Open-Meteo sees the request as `name=Cafe+`, `+Bar+Tokyo=` (empty), and silently returns no result. Same class of silent correctness loss as #164 (dashboard latency URL drift) but for end-user input.

All three concerns share `http_get` and the three callers (`geocode`, `fetch_weather`, `fetch_forecast`). Fixing them together gives one focused PR over one file family.

### Steps to reproduce

Three independent reproducers — each demonstrates a different concern.

**1. Hung Open-Meteo blocks the chat task forever (timeout):**

```bash
# Spawn a fake Open-Meteo listener on port 80 that accepts and hangs:
sudo python3 - <<'PY' &
import socket, time
s = socket.socket(); s.setsockopt(socket.SOL_SOCKET, socket.SO_REUSEADDR, 1)
s.bind(("127.0.0.1", 80)); s.listen(1)
while True:
    c, _ = s.accept(); time.sleep(600); c.close()
PY

# Point Open-Meteo lookups at 127.0.0.1 via /etc/hosts, then ask for weather:
echo "127.0.0.1 api.open-meteo.com geocoding-api.open-meteo.com" | sudo tee -a /etc/hosts
curl -m 120 -X POST http://127.0.0.1:3000/api/chat \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"message":"what is the weather in Denver"}'
# → curl gives up after 120 s; genie-core's tool-dispatch task hangs forever.
```

**2. Oversized response grows genie-core's RSS:** point the daemon at a mock server that streams unlimited bytes after the headers; `http_get`'s body loop accumulates into a `String` with no cap.

**3. URL-encoding gap (silent wrong answer):**

```bash
# Ask for a location containing reserved URL chars:
curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:3000/api/chat \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"message":"weather in Q&A Cafe Tokyo"}'
# → the geocode HTTP request line is:
#   GET /v1/search?name=Q&A+Cafe+Tokyo&count=1&language=en&format=json
#   (Open-Meteo parses the `&A+Cafe+Tokyo` as a separate query param;
#    `name=Q` is the only useful piece; result is "location not found"
#    or the wrong city named "Q".)
```

### Expected behavior

- `http_get` enforces a TOTAL request-lifecycle deadline (default 15 s), not just on `TcpStream::connect`. A hung Open-Meteo surfaces as `anyhow!("Open-Meteo {} timed out after Ns")` within the budget, never an indefinite hang.
- The body-read loop has a maximum size (default 1 MiB) so a misbehaving Open-Meteo can never grow RSS without bound. Exceeding the cap surfaces a clear `anyhow!("Open-Meteo response exceeded N bytes")`.
- The function refuses `Transfer-Encoding: chunked` with an explicit `Err`, instead of silently corrupting the body via the broken heuristic at lines 216-225. (Open-Meteo's free-tier API doesn't actually use chunked encoding — the fallback decoder was always defense-in-depth that did more harm than good.)
- Location queries percent-encode every RFC 3986 reserved character (`&`, `?`, `#`, `=`, `%`, `+`, ` `, `/`, `<`, `>`, `"`, `'`) before going into the query string. The example query string for *"Q&A Cafe Tokyo"* becomes `name=Q%26A%20Cafe%20Tokyo&count=1&language=en&format=json`. Open-Meteo's geocoder receives the intended name.
- Default `geniepod.toml` and `geniepod.dev.toml` see no behaviour change for healthy Open-Meteo and ASCII / space-only location names.

### Actual behavior

- Lines 182-189 wrap `TcpStream::connect` in a 10-second `tokio::time::timeout`. The subsequent `writer.write_all` (line 197), the body loop's `buf_reader.read_line` (line 205), and the post-loop chunked-fallback (lines 216-225) all run with no timeout, no size cap, and no `Transfer-Encoding` validation.

- The "chunked decoder" at lines 216-225 looks plausible but is wrong on two axes: (a) joining non-hex lines with `decoded.push_str(line);` skips any inter-line content separator (a chunked JSON object spanning two chunks ends up as one long line with no whitespace between the closing brace of chunk 1 and the opening brace of chunk 2), and (b) the heuristic `body.starts_with(|c: char| c.is_ascii_hexdigit())` fires on any response whose first body character is a hex digit — including a perfectly valid JSON response whose first key happens to be a hex-looking string.

- Line 71 `location.replace(' ', "+")` only handles ASCII spaces. The query string is constructed via `format!("/v1/search?name={}&count=1&language=en&format=json", encoded)`, so any `&` in the user's location terminates the `name` parameter at the HTTP level. Open-Meteo's geocoder returns "location not found" or matches whatever the truncated `name` resolves to. The user sees "I couldn't find the weather for *Q&A Cafe Tokyo*" with no diagnostic.

### Hardware

Non-Jetson (x86_64 dev / cross-build host) and Jetson alike — the bug is in pure tokio I/O scheduling and string formatting, no hardware path. Triggered by anything that pauses Open-Meteo's HTTP response after the TCP handshake, by sending an unbounded body, OR by any user / LLM query whose location string contains reserved URL characters.

### JetPack / L4T version

_No response_ — bug is hardware-independent.

### GenieClaw version / commit

`main @ f821eda` (upstream HEAD as of filing) / `crates/genie-core/src/tools/weather.rs:65-228`. Never touched since the workspace scaffold commit `0a7363b`.

### Relevant logs

```shell
# Hung Open-Meteo scenario — chat task hangs, no diagnostic from genie-core
$ journalctl -u genie-core --since '5 minutes ago' | tail -15
… genie-core[12345]: INFO  POST /api/chat origin=Api status=in_progress
…
# (no further output until either the client gives up OR /etc/hosts is reverted)
```

```text
# URL-encoding gap — wrong geocode request sent on the wire (tcpdump on lo or eth0)
$ sudo tcpdump -i lo -A 'port 80 and host geocoding-api.open-meteo.com' -c 5
GET /v1/search?name=Q&A+Cafe+Tokyo&count=1&language=en&format=json HTTP/1.1
Host: geocoding-api.open-meteo.com:80
User-Agent: GeniePod/0.2
Accept: application/json
Connection: close

# Open-Meteo's response:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
…
{"generationtime_ms":0.058}    # empty — no "results" key — "location not found"
```

### Additional context

**Symptom users see:**
1. *"The assistant froze on a weather question."* Triggered any time Open-Meteo throttles, returns a slow response, or has a brief outage — happens to all free public APIs on a recurring basis.
2. *"Why can't Genie find weather for X?"* For any household whose place name contains `&` (`"Q&A Cafe"`, `"Mom & Pop Diner"`, *"Cafe & Bar near Tokyo"*), or transliterations involving `+` (some German / Romance compound names), or `#` (rare but used in some place names), the geocoder silently fails. Operators have no way to tell from the log whether the input was malformed, the API was down, or the location truly doesn't exist.
3. *Unbounded memory growth on a misbehaving / hostile upstream.* The plain-HTTP connection means a network attacker can feed unlimited bytes into the response loop. The accumulated `body` string grows without limit until OOM-kill.

**Why the existing tests don't catch it:**
- `crates/genie-core/src/tools/weather.rs::tests` (lines 253-263) covers only `wmo_code_to_description`. There's no test of `http_get`, `geocode`, `fetch_weather`, or any failure-mode coverage at all.
- The 3 `parse_http_url_*` tests in `ha/client.rs` and the 5 new regressions in PR #174 are the closest precedent for the right test shape (mock `TcpListener` against `http_get`); none of them reach into `weather.rs`.

**Files to touch:**
- `crates/genie-core/src/tools/weather.rs`:
  - New constants near the top: `WEATHER_CONNECT_TIMEOUT = 10s`, `WEATHER_REQUEST_TIMEOUT = 15s`, `WEATHER_MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES = 1 MiB`.
  - `http_get` (lines 182-228) refactored: use the new connect-timeout; wrap the post-connect block in `tokio::time::timeout(WEATHER_REQUEST_TIMEOUT, …)` and map `Elapsed` to a clear `anyhow!`; cap body accumulation with a check-and-bail when total bytes would exceed `WEATHER_MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES`; remove the broken chunked-encoding heuristic at lines 216-225 and instead detect `Transfer-Encoding: chunked` in the headers and bail with an explicit unsupported-encoding `Err`.
  - New helper `fn url_encode_query_param(s: &str) -> String` that percent-encodes every RFC 3986 reserved character + non-ASCII bytes. Hand-rolled, no new dependency. ~15 lines.
  - Update `geocode` (line 71) and the two callers in `fetch_weather` / `fetch_forecast` (lines 105 and 150 — wherever the location is interpolated) to use the helper instead of `location.replace(' ', "+")`.

- `crates/genie-core/src/tools/weather.rs::tests` — 5 new regressions, all hardware-free:
  - `url_encode_query_param_percent_encodes_reserved_chars` — direct helper coverage: ASCII pass-through; `&`, `#`, `=`, `+`, `?`, `%`, spaces; multi-byte UTF-8 (e.g. `"München"` → `M%C3%BCnchen`).
  - `hung_server_after_connect_times_out_cleanly` — mock `TcpListener` that accepts then sleeps forever; assert `http_get` returns `Err` containing "timed out" inside the test budget (e.g. 300 ms with a millisecond-scale test override).
  - `oversized_response_is_size_capped` — mock listener that writes a valid 200 header then streams bytes past the cap; assert clean `Err` containing "exceeded N bytes".
  - `chunked_encoding_is_explicitly_rejected` — mock listener that returns `Transfer-Encoding: chunked` with one chunk; assert `Err` containing "chunked encoding not supported" (instead of the silent body corruption today).
  - `geocode_request_line_contains_percent_encoded_location` — mock listener that captures the raw request bytes; call `geocode("Q&A Cafe Tokyo")`; assert the captured request line contains `name=Q%26A` and does NOT contain `name=Q&A`.

**Acceptance (PR):**
- `cargo test -p genie-core --lib tools::weather` — the 1 existing test still passes, 5 new tests pass.
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy -D warnings`, `cargo test --workspace --no-default-features` — all green on both feature axes.
- Pointing `genie-core` at a `kill -STOP`ped fake Open-Meteo returns an explicit `Err("Open-Meteo … timed out after 15s")` within ~15 s — no daemon hang.
- A chat turn for *"weather in Q&A Cafe Tokyo"* produces a request line containing `name=Q%26A%20Cafe%20Tokyo` (verifiable via `tcpdump` if the operator has a real Open-Meteo) — the geocoder returns a hit instead of empty.
- Default `geniepod.toml` and `geniepod.dev.toml` see byte-identical behaviour for healthy Open-Meteo and ASCII / space-only location names (regression on the working path).

**Timing:** Standalone — no upstream PRs touch `weather.rs`. Lands independently of any open PR. No merge-conflict surface with PR #176 (skills signature), PR #182 (LLM timeouts), PR #192 (https service URLs), PR #202 (health check_http).

**Real Behavior Proof:** `cargo test -p genie-core --lib tools::weather` showing the 5 new tests pass alongside the existing `wmo_codes` test. Plus optional manual proof: spawn a mock listener that hangs, assert `http_get` returns `Err` in ~15 s instead of hanging.

**Related:**
- **#173 / PR #174 (mine)** — fixed the same unbounded-read pattern in `ha/client.rs`. This report is the sibling fix in `tools/weather.rs`.
- **#181 / PR #182** (open) — fixes timeouts on the OpenAI-compat LLM client. Different file (`llm/openai_compat.rs`), different protocol nuances. The three (HA, LLM, Weather) share the "raw-TCP HTTP client with insufficient read timeouts" theme; landing all three would close the "unbounded read" class workspace-wide.
- **#185 / PR #202** (open) — rejects non-HTTP TCP responses in `genie-health::check_http`. Different concern (validity of the response shape, not the request lifecycle).
- **#164 / PR #165** — silent correctness loss from a magic-literal URL drift. The URL-encoding gap reported here is the same silent-correctness class, in a different code path.
